figures.caption: Table 1: Weco-Kaggle Lite results for AGENTGA benchmark runs†. Rankings are from actual Kaggle submissions and use the private leaderboard. † Results reported for 10/16 Weco-Kaggle Lite competitions.
Figure 4: Parent-child structure for the tabular-playground-series-aug-2022 run, showing explicit reuse of inherited archives.

Figure 2: Tournament outcomes by task across these benchmark runs. Each bar shows the share of parent-child tournaments won or lost by the child, with counts using all tournaments that had an elite parent. De novo Initial proposals lose overwhelmingly, while parent-conditioned operators are substantially more competitive.
Figure 1: Two-layer architecture. Evolution assigns a task type and parent archives to each fresh autonomous run; the resulting child competes only against its corresponding elite parent.

Table 3: Representative positioning of AGENTGA. Primary Unit denotes what the outer system selects and reuses: an artifact, a bounded agentic execution, or an autonomous run. Orchestrated indicates whether a higher-level search coordinates multiple runs. Agent-seed indicates whether the optimized object is the initial state that launches a fresh autonomous run.
Table 2: Complete tool suite available to AGENTGA agents.
Table 5: Five experiments from one initial tabular-playground-series-aug-2022 agent invocation (Iteration 1, Population 1).
Table 4: Weco-Kaggle Lite benchmark: 16 Kaggle tabular ML competitions from Jiang et al. [2025].
Figure 3: Best-score progression over the tabular-playground-series-aug-2022 run.
